pattern: Record each letter's last occurrence, then sweep left to right extending the current part's end to the farthest last-position seen; close a part the moment the scan index meets that end.
hints: A part cannot end before the final appearance of any letter it already contains.
Precompute, for every letter, the index of its last occurrence in the string.
Scan once tracking the maximum last-occurrence among letters seen so far; when the cursor reaches that maximum, cut a part and start the next from the following index.

edge_inputs.description: three-part split from the worked example
all distinct letters each become their own part
all identical letters form one part
outer letter wraps an inner block into one part

stress.description: random lowercase strings over a small alphabet to force overlaps
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:rng.randint(1, 8)]
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: small alphabet maximises letter reuse and partition merging

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def partitionLabels(self, s):
        n = len(s)
        res = []
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            far = s.rfind(s[i])
            j = i
            while j < far:
                j += 1
                far = max(far, s.rfind(s[j]))
            res.append(j - i + 1)
            i = j + 1
        return res
